verdict_reasoning: SRC encodes a non-receptor tyrosine kinase that is canonically inner-leaflet anchored at the cytoplasmic face of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. Its catalytic and SH2/SH3 domains face the cytoplasm, not the extracellular space. However, multiple lines of evidence support a contextual surface story: (1) cancer cells display ecto-SRC on the outer leaflet — documented in several tumor types as a cell-state-induced, oncogenic-transformation-associated ecto-kinase accessible to antibody binding; (2) cell-death-related exposure on apoptotic/ICD-dying cells has been reported for related inner-leaflet kinases; (3) no GPI anchor, no TM domain, no stable extracellular complex partner at baseline. The baseline localization is inner-leaflet-anchored (cytoplasmic face), but the documented cancer-cell ecto-pool qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced. The protein body does NOT reach the outer leaflet by its own mechanism under normal conditions.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and reproducibility of the ecto-SRC cancer-cell surface pool; some reports may reflect non-specific binding rather than stable outer-leaflet display.
